sections.title: Organization Details
sections.description: Basic information about your organization and IT infrastructure.

questions.text: Company Name
Industry Type
Number of Employees
IT Infrastructure Type

sections.title: Security Controls
sections.description: Evaluate the core security measures protecting your systems.

questions.text: Do you use a firewall?
questions.recommendation: Deploy a network firewall to protect against external threats and unauthorized access.

questions.text: Is antivirus/EDR installed on all systems?
questions.recommendation: Install enterprise-grade antivirus/EDR solution across all endpoints.

questions.text: Do you use Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
questions.recommendation: Enable MFA immediately — it prevents over 99% of account compromise attacks.

questions.text: Is sensitive data encrypted at rest and in transit?
questions.recommendation: Implement end-to-end encryption for all sensitive data storage and transmission.

questions.text: Do you enforce strong password policies?
questions.recommendation: Enforce password complexity, minimum length, and rotation policies.

questions.text: Are access controls role-based (RBAC)?
questions.recommendation: Implement Role-Based Access Control to enforce least-privilege principles.

questions.text: Is network traffic monitored continuously?
questions.recommendation: Deploy network monitoring tools to detect anomalous traffic patterns.

sections.title: Access & Identity Management
sections.description: Assess how user identities and access privileges are managed.

questions.text: Are inactive accounts removed or disabled regularly?
questions.recommendation: Implement automated account lifecycle management to disable inactive accounts.

questions.text: Are all admin activities logged and audited?
questions.recommendation: Enable comprehensive admin activity logging with tamper-proof audit trails.

questions.text: Is privilege escalation monitored and alerted?
questions.recommendation: Implement PAM (Privileged Access Management) to monitor and control privilege escalation.

sections.title: Patch & Update Management
sections.description: Evaluate how your organization manages system updates and security patches.

questions.text: How often are systems and software updated?
questions.recommendation: Establish a regular patch management schedule — outdated systems are a primary attack vector.

questions.text: Are critical security patches applied immediately upon release?
questions.recommendation: Implement an emergency patch process for critical CVEs with SLA under 24 hours.

questions.text: Is there an automated patch/update management system?
questions.recommendation: Deploy automated patch management tools (e.g., WSUS, Ansible, Intune) to reduce manual overhead.

sections.title: Incident History
sections.description: Review past security incidents and your organization's response capabilities.

questions.text: Has your organization experienced security breaches or data leaks?
questions.recommendation: Conduct a thorough post-incident review and implement a corrective action plan.

questions.text: Number of security incidents in the past 12 months
Do you have a documented Incident Response Plan (IRP)?
questions.recommendation: Develop and test a formal Incident Response Plan aligned with NIST SP 800-61.

questions.text: Are incidents documented, reviewed, and used for improvement?
questions.recommendation: Establish a lessons-learned process to improve security posture after each incident.

sections.title: Data Sensitivity & Protection
sections.description: Assess how sensitive data is classified, stored, and protected.

questions.text: What is the highest classification of data your organization handles?
Is data backed up regularly and tested for restoration?
questions.recommendation: Implement automated daily backups with regular restoration testing (3-2-1 backup rule).

questions.text: Are backups encrypted and stored securely offsite?
questions.recommendation: Encrypt all backups and store copies in geographically separate, secure locations.

questions.text: Do you have a formal data retention and disposal policy?
questions.recommendation: Define and enforce data retention schedules with secure disposal procedures.

sections.title: Compliance & Governance
sections.description: Evaluate adherence to regulatory standards and internal governance practices.

questions.text: Which compliance standards does your organization follow?
questions.recommendation: Adopt a recognized security framework (ISO 27001 or NIST CSF) to structure your security program.

questions.text: Are internal or external security audits conducted regularly?
questions.recommendation: Schedule annual external audits and quarterly internal reviews.

questions.text: Do employees receive regular security awareness training?
questions.recommendation: Implement mandatory annual security awareness training with phishing simulations.

sections.title: Network & Infrastructure
sections.description: Review the security architecture of your network and infrastructure.

questions.text: Is network segmentation (VLANs/DMZ) implemented?
questions.recommendation: Implement network segmentation to contain breaches and limit lateral movement.

questions.text: Are Intrusion Detection/Prevention Systems (IDS/IPS) deployed?
questions.recommendation: Deploy IDS/IPS to detect and block malicious network activity in real-time.

questions.text: Are open ports and services regularly audited and minimized?
questions.recommendation: Conduct regular port scans and close all unnecessary open ports and services.

questions.text: Is VPN or Zero Trust used for all remote access?
questions.recommendation: Enforce VPN or Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) for all remote connections.

sections.title: Advanced Security Practices
sections.description: Assess proactive and advanced security capabilities.

questions.text: Do you perform regular vulnerability scanning?
questions.recommendation: Schedule automated vulnerability scans weekly and remediate critical findings within 72 hours.

questions.text: Are penetration tests conducted at least annually?
questions.recommendation: Engage a qualified third-party for annual penetration testing to identify exploitable weaknesses.

questions.text: Are security logs monitored in real-time?
questions.recommendation: Implement real-time log monitoring with automated alerting for suspicious events.

questions.text: Do you use SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) tools?
questions.recommendation: Deploy a SIEM solution (e.g., Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel) for centralized threat detection.
